#### 2_Ocurrences

RUN FIRST THE THREE FOLLOWING CHUNKS

All occurrence datasets located under `data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/` were automatically discovered and processed contributor by contributor. For each dataset file (.csv, .parquet, .rds, .rda, .RData), the script `01_clean_occurrences.Rmd`

(i) standardized taxonomic rank information (`taxonRank`),
(ii) inferred rank from `scientificName` when missing,
(iii) generated normalized binomial names, and
(iv) extracted species-level and infraspecific taxa for comparison against the current locked EpIG backbone version.

Backbone matching was performed on unique binomials using `match_species_backbone()`, returning a match code (0–3) indicating **exact matches**, **fuzzy matches**, or **taxa not present in the backbone**. For each dataset, two outputs were generated:

-   an augmented dataset (**\*\_epi**) preserving all original records while adding standardized taxonomic and epiphyte-review columns, and

-   a review table (**\*\_backbone_review.csv**) containing unique binomials, match codes, and review flags.

A global summary table (`summary_epi_backbone.csv`) was written at the root directory (*see below*), reporting dataset-level metrics including total rows, number of binomials, unique binomials, proportion of binomial records, and counts of each match category. This table serves as the decision matrix for prioritizing manual taxonomic revision. Each new dataset summary is added there.

1.  PHASE 1 — Epiphyte-focused datasets
2.  PHASE 2 — Mixed and mid-Sized datasets
3.  PHASE 3 — Large generalist aggregators

**IMPORTANT INFORMATION!!**

\*backbone_review is a filter of the unique species and the Match with the backbone

Remember to always ***filter*** only `ok_for_analysis` & epiphytes (`Match = 1`) & `Neotropics`

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(sf)
library(rWCVP)
library(CoordinateCleaner)
library(ggplot2)

summary_rev <- read.csv("./data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/summary_epi_backbone.csv")
```

load Neotropics, WFO and add helpers for occurrences

```{r}
# Helpers and functions

extract_genus <- function(x) {
  x <- str_squish(as.character(x))
  out <- str_extract(x, "^(x)?[A-Z][A-Za-z-]+")
  out[out == ""] <- NA_character_
  out
}

norm_genus_key <- function(x) {
  x <- str_squish(as.character(x))
  x <- str_replace_all(x, "[\u00D7]", "x")
  x <- str_replace_all(x, "^x\\s+", "x")
  x <- str_extract(x, "^(x)?[A-Za-z-]+")
  x <- ifelse(is.na(x), NA_character_, x)
  str_to_lower(x)
}

norm_family_key <- function(x) {
  x <- str_squish(as.character(x))
  x[x == ""] <- NA_character_
  str_to_lower(x)
}

norm_name_key <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    stringr::str_to_lower() %>%
    stringr::str_squish()
}

author_map <- backbone$backbone %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    sci_key = stringr::str_to_lower(stringr::str_squish(ABBREVIAT)),
    AUTHOR  = stringr::str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  dplyr::distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)

append_change_tag <- function(changes, tag) {
  changes <- as.character(changes)
  empty <- is.na(changes) | str_squish(changes) == ""
  out <- changes
  out[empty] <- tag
  out[!empty] <- paste(out[!empty], tag, sep = " | ")
  out
}

drop_epig_cols <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    select(
      -matches("^matchCodeEpig(\\.|$)"),
      -matches("^matchedNameRawEpig(\\.|$)"),
      -matches("^matchedNameEpig(\\.|$)"),
      -matches("^synonymEpig(\\.|$)"),
      -matches("^matchSimEpig(\\.|$)")
    )
}

make_genus_lookup <- function(wfo_df) {
  if (!"taxonRank" %in% names(wfo_df)) wfo_df$taxonRank <- NA_character_

  wfo_df %>%
    transmute(
      genus_key = norm_genus_key(genus),
      family = str_squish(family),
      taxonRank = str_to_lower(str_squish(taxonRank))
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(genus_key), genus_key != "") %>%
    arrange((is.na(family) | family == ""), !(taxonRank %in% "genus")) %>%
    distinct(genus_key, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    select(genus_key, family)
}

make_family_lookup <- function() {
  data("taxonomic_mapping", package = "rWCVP")

  taxonomic_mapping %>%
    mutate(family_key = norm_family_key(family)) %>%
    distinct(family_key, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    select(family_key, order, higher)
}

fill_taxonomy <- function(df, genus_lookup, family_lookup) {

  if (!"genus" %in% names(df)) df$genus <- NA_character_
  if (!"family" %in% names(df)) df$family <- NA_character_
  if (!"order" %in% names(df)) df$order <- NA_character_

  df %>%
    mutate(
      genus = dplyr::coalesce(extract_genus(scientificName), genus),
      genus_key = norm_genus_key(genus)
    ) %>%
    left_join(
      genus_lookup %>% dplyr::rename(family_lookup = family),
      by = "genus_key"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      family = dplyr::coalesce(family, family_lookup)
    ) %>%
    select(-genus_key, -family_lookup) %>%
    mutate(
      family_key = norm_family_key(family)
    ) %>%
    left_join(
      family_lookup %>% dplyr::rename(order_lookup = order),
      by = "family_key"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      order = dplyr::coalesce(order, order_lookup)
    ) %>%
    select(-family_key, -order_lookup)
}


# WFO
wfo_classification_path <- file.choose()
wfo_raw <- readr::read_tsv(
  wfo_classification_path,
  col_types = cols(.default = "c")
)

genus_lookup <- make_genus_lookup(wfo_raw)
family_lookup <- make_family_lookup()


# Neotropics
morrone_shp_path <- file.choose()
morrone_nt <- sf::st_read(morrone_shp_path, quiet = TRUE) %>%
  sf::st_make_valid() %>%
  sf::st_transform(4326)


# Final filter used to export (common columns + `ok_for_analysis` + epiphytes with `Match = 1` + Neotropics)
prepare_occ_for_review <- function(df, morrone_nt) {

  # keep only records flagged as suitable for analysis
  df_out <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      matchCodeEpig = as.character(matchCodeEpig),
      .ok_for_analysis = dplyr::case_when(
        as.character(.ok_for_analysis) %in% c("TRUE", "True", "true", "T", "1") ~ TRUE,
        TRUE ~ FALSE
      ),
      decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude)),
      decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude))
    ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(
      .ok_for_analysis,
      matchCodeEpig == "1"
    )

  # keep only records located in the Neotropics (Morrone polygons)
  if (all(c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude") %in% names(df_out))) {

    pts_valid <- df_out %>%
      dplyr::filter(
        !is.na(decimalLongitude),
        !is.na(decimalLatitude)
      ) %>%
      sf::st_as_sf(
        coords = c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude"),
        crs = 4326,
        remove = FALSE
      )

    pts_nt <- sf::st_join(
      pts_valid,
      morrone_nt,
      join = sf::st_intersects,
      left = FALSE
    )

    df_out <- pts_nt %>%
      sf::st_drop_geometry()
  }

  df_out
}

# clean line breaks for Stephan
clean_linebreaks <- function(x) {
  x |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      dplyr::across(
        where(is.character),
        ~ stringr::str_replace_all(., "[\r\n]", " ")
      )
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      dplyr::across(
        where(is.character),
        stringr::str_squish
      )
    )
}
```

##### \[EXAMPLE\] Database INABIO

Location: \[path\] Who & what: Example run by GLM \[07/02/2026\] to test the search function.

```{r}
# Read list/dataset/or similar
spp_list <- read.csv("./temp/species_INABIO_species_and_infraspecies.csv")

# Whatever the previous form, the final form should be:
spp_list <- spp_list[1]
head(spp_list)
str(spp_list)

# Running the steps
inabio <- match_species_backbone(spp_list, backbone$backbone,
                              spp_col = "species")

# Inspect results
head(inabio)
table(inabio$match_code)

# Retain accepted name only
inabio_acc <- attach_accepted_name(inabio, backbone$backbone)

# Inspect results
inabio %>% filter(match_code==1) %>% head()
inabio_acc %>% filter(match_code==1) %>% head()

# Further revisions and changes should be included in the same chunk
```

####Phase 1: epiphyte focused datasets

`Check all species in the review file`

##### 1. Database HIP2025

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/HIP2025/HIP2025.csv\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[27/03/2026\].

No records were retained with Match = 1 after the EPIG backbone filtering step.

This dataset contains parasitic or climbing taxa (e.g. *Misodendrum*, *Luzuriaga*), so it does not need revision for the epiphyte workflow.

No need for follow up

##### 2. Database AssisI – assis_i_2026_literature

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/AssisI/assis_i_2026_literature.csv\]

Who & what: check all fields run by MJCH \[27/03/2026\] listing the names to check run by MJCH \[28/03/2026\]

-----TODO check names----------------------------------

```{r check_all_fields}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)

db <- read.csv("data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/AssisI/assis_i_2026_literature.csv")

str(db)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank) # ok. has species and variety

# kingdom, phylum and class
# empty in the original raw file
# not a concern for now because they are not part of the selected final common columns

# fill order, family and phyllum if empty
db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
    genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
    family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
  mutate(
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) #ok
# author from backbone
author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
    sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
    AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship
db <- db %>% mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone

# Geography ------------

# country and countryCode are present
# locality is present and useful

# stateProvince
# empty in the raw original file

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude
library(CoordinateCleaner)
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
# empty in the raw original file
# expected for a literature compilation

# minimumElevationInMeters
# empty in the raw original file

# verbatimElevation
# empty in the raw original file

# this is expected for a literature compilation and not a priority now

# Dates -------------

# eventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
# empty in the raw original file

# no date information seems to be available
# this is expected for this literature-based dataset

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
# Derived from Hemiepiphyte_* and Epiphyte_* fields. If a species is reported as epiphyte in any source, it is classified as "E". Hemiepiphyte ("H") is assigned only when no epiphyte evidence is present. habitat_bf used as fallback.
# Categories follow internal data dictionary: E = epiphyte, H = hemiepiphyte.
db <- db %>%
  mutate(lifeForm = case_when(
      # 1. Epiphyte takes priority if reported anywhere
      Epiphyte_bf == 1 | Epiphyte_zotz == 1 ~ "E",
      # 2. Hemiepiphyte only if no epiphyte evidence
      Hemiepiphyte_bf == 1 | Hemiepiphyte_zotz == 1 ~ "H",
      # 3. Fallback using habitat text
      stringr::str_detect(habitat_bf, regex("epiph", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "E",
      stringr::str_detect(habitat_bf, regex("hemi", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "H",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
# append changes
db <- db %>%
  mutate(changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0(
        "lifeForm assigned (E prioritized over H; derived from bf/zotz and habitat fallback) [",
        Sys.Date(),
        "]")))

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode is present
# probably refers to source institution / cited institution in the literature

# collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber
# empty in the raw original file
# expected for a literature compilation rather than herbarium specimen data

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) 
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    basisOfRecord = as.character(basisOfRecord),
    basisOfRecord = dplyr::coalesce(dplyr::na_if(basisOfRecord, ""), "MaterialCitation"),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing basisOfRecord with 'MaterialCitation' [", Sys.Date(), "]")
    )
  )

# associatedReferences is present
glimpse(unique(db$associatedReferences)) #looks ok
sum(is.na(db$associatedReferences) | db$associatedReferences == "")

# bibliographicCitation is present and useful for traceability


# final filter --------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)


# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/AssisI/assis_i_2026_literature.csv", na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/assis_i_2026_literature.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
db %>%
  mutate(matchCodeEpig = as.character(matchCodeEpig)) %>%
  count(matchCodeEpig, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

#   matchCodeEpig    n
# 1             0   29 # 29 records; 23 species
# 2             1 1029 # 97% accepted names in epig
# 3          <NA>    1 # empty row

db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

##### 3. Database AssisI – assis_i_2026_specieslink

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/AssisI/assis_i_2026_specieslink.csv\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[28/03/2026\] Check all fields by MJCH \[28/03/2026\] check_EpIG_taxo

```{r check_all_fields}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)

db <- read.csv("data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/AssisI/assis_i_2026_specieslink.csv")

str(db)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank) # ok. has species

# kingdom, phylum ok

# order, family 
db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
    genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
    family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
  mutate(
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) #ok
# author from backbone
author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
    sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
    AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship
db <- db %>% mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone

# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
db <- db %>% mutate(
    country = case_when(
      country %in% c("Brasil", "BRASIL") ~ "Brazil",
      TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
db <- db %>% mutate(countryCode = case_when(
      country == "Brazil" ~ "BR",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))

# stateProvince
db <- db %>% dplyr::mutate(stateProvince = stateprovince)

# locality is present and useful

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude
db <- db %>% dplyr::mutate(
    decimalLongitude = longitude,
    decimalLatitude  = latitude)
#clean coordinates
library(CoordinateCleaner)
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
db <- db %>% dplyr::mutate(
    coordinateUncertaintyInMeters = coordinateprecision)

# minimumElevationInMeters
db <- db %>% dplyr::mutate(
    minimumElevationInMeters = minimumelevation)

# verbatimElevation
# empty in the raw original file

# Dates -------------

# eventDate
db <- db %>% mutate(
    yearcollected  = as.integer(yearcollected),
    monthcollected = as.integer(monthcollected),
    daycollected   = as.integer(daycollected),
    eventDate = case_when(
      !is.na(yearcollected) &
      !is.na(monthcollected) &
      !is.na(daycollected) ~
        paste0(
          yearcollected, "-",
          str_pad(monthcollected, 2, pad = "0"), "-",
          str_pad(daycollected,   2, pad = "0")),
      TRUE ~ eventDate))

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
db <- db %>% dplyr::mutate(
    year = yearcollected)

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
# Derived from Hemiepiphyte_* and Epiphyte_* fields. If a species is reported as epiphyte in any source, it is classified as "E". Hemiepiphyte ("H") is assigned only when no epiphyte evidence is present. habitat_bf used as fallback.
# Categories follow internal data dictionary: E = epiphyte, H = hemiepiphyte.
db <- db %>%
  mutate(lifeForm = case_when(
      # 1. Epiphyte takes priority if reported anywhere
      Epiphyte_bf == 1 | Epiphyte_zotz == 1 ~ "E",
      # 2. Hemiepiphyte only if no epiphyte evidence
      Hemiepiphyte_bf == 1 | Hemiepiphyte_zotz == 1 ~ "H",
      # 3. Fallback using habitat text
      stringr::str_detect(habitat_bf, regex("epiph", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "E",
      stringr::str_detect(habitat_bf, regex("hemi", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "H",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
# append changes
db <- db %>%
  mutate(changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0(
        "lifeForm assigned (E prioritized over H; derived from bf/zotz and habitat fallback) [",
        Sys.Date(),
        "]")))

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber filled with a function

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord)
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    basisOfRecord = as.character(basisOfRecord),
    basisOfRecord = dplyr::coalesce(dplyr::na_if(basisOfRecord, ""), "PreservedSpecimen"),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing basisOfRecord with 'PreservedSpecimen' [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# associatedReferences 
# not necessary

# bibliographicCitation 


# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)


# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/AssisI/assis_i_2026_specieslink.csv", na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/assis_i_2026_specieslink.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
db %>%
  mutate(matchCodeEpig = as.character(matchCodeEpig)) %>%
  count(matchCodeEpig, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

##### 4. Database AssisI – assis_i_2026_af

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/AssisI/assis_i_2026_af\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[28/03/2026\] Check all fields by MJCH \[28/03/2026\] check_EpIG_taxo

```{r check_all_fields}
db <- read.csv("data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/AssisI/assis_i_2026_af.csv")

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family 
db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
    genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
    family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
  mutate(
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) #ok
# author from backbone
author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
    sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
    AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship
db <- db %>% mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone

# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country) #ok

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode)
db$countryCode <- toupper(
  countrycode(trimws(db$country),
              origin = "country.name",
              destination = "iso2c",
              warn = TRUE))

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince)

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # it looks ok

# verbatimElevation
# empty in the raw original file

# Dates -------------

# eventDate 
# empty in the raw original file

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
# empty in the raw original file

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
# Derived from Hemiepiphyte_* and Epiphyte_* fields. If a species is reported as epiphyte in any source, it is classified as "E". Hemiepiphyte ("H") is assigned only when no epiphyte evidence is present. habitat_bf used as fallback.
# Categories follow internal data dictionary: E = epiphyte, H = hemiepiphyte.
db <- db %>%
  mutate(lifeForm = case_when(
      # 1. Epiphyte takes priority if reported anywhere
      Epiphyte_bf == 1 | Epiphyte_zotz == 1 ~ "E",
      # 2. Hemiepiphyte only if no epiphyte evidence
      Hemiepiphyte_bf == 1 | Hemiepiphyte_zotz == 1 ~ "H",
      # 3. Fallback using habitat text
      stringr::str_detect(habitat_bf, regex("epiph", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "E",
      stringr::str_detect(habitat_bf, regex("hemi", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "H",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
# append changes
db <- db %>%
  mutate(changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0(
        "lifeForm assigned (E prioritized over H; derived from bf/zotz and habitat fallback) [",
        Sys.Date(),
        "]")))

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber empty

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord)
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    basisOfRecord = as.character(basisOfRecord),
    basisOfRecord = dplyr::coalesce(dplyr::na_if(basisOfRecord, ""), "PreservedSpecimen"),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing basisOfRecord with 'PreservedSpecimen' [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# associatedReferences, bibliographicCitation empty


# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/AssisI/assis_i_2026_af.csv", na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/assis_i_2026_af.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_all_fields}
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # it looks ok

# verbatimElevation
# empty in the raw original file

# Dates -------------

# eventDate 
glimpse(db$eventDate)
db$eventDate <- as.numeric(dplyr::na_if(db$eventDate, ""))
db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, origin = "1899-12-30")

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # empty

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber empty

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) #ok

# associatedReferences ok

# bibliographicCitation empty


# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/zuluaga_a_2021.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
```

##### 8. Database JimenezDA – jimenez_d_2023a

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/JimenezDA/jimenez_d_2023_chiapas.csv\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[28/03/2026\] Check all fields by MJCH \[09/04/2026\] check_EpIG_taxo

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/JimenezDA/jimenez_d_2023_chiapas.csv"
db <- read.csv(path)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
  TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
)
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok

# scientificNameAuthorship
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship) #ok

# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country) #ok

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # it looks ok

# verbatimElevation
# empty in the raw original file

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty
# glimpse(db$eventDate)
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, origin = "1899-12-30")

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
# db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # empty

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) #ok

# associatedReferences empty

# bibliographicCitation empty



### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))




# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/jimenez_d_2023_chiapas.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
  count(matchCodeEpig, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

##### 9. Database KroemerT

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/KroemerT/kroemer_t_2024.csv\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[28/03/2026\] Check all fields by MJCH \[28/03/2026\] check_EpIG_taxo

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/KroemerT/kroemer_t_2024.csv"
db <- read.csv(path)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family 
db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
    genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
    family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
  mutate(
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship) 
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) 
# author from backbone
author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
    sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
    AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship
db <- db %>% mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country) #ok

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
# hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # it looks ok

# verbatimElevation
# empty in the raw original file

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty
glimpse(db$eventDate)
# si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) 
db <- db %>% mutate(
    lifeForm = case_when(
      lifeForm == "holoepiphyte" ~ "E",
      lifeForm == "nomadic vine" ~ "NV",
      TRUE ~ lifeForm))
unique(db$lifeForm) 

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) 
db <- db %>% mutate(
    basisOfRecord = case_when(
      basisOfRecord == "herbarium specimen" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
      TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences empty

# bibliographicCitation empty


# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/kroemer_t_2024.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

db %>%

  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

```{r check_all_fields}
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
#   select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country) 
# to English
db <- db %>% mutate(
    country = case_when(
      country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
      TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # it looks ok

# verbatimElevation
# empty in the raw original file

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty ok
glimpse(db$eventDate)
# si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
# glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
# db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm)
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    lifeForm = case_when(
      lifeForm %in% c("Hemiepífita", "Hemiepifita") ~ "H",
      lifeForm %in% c("Liana") ~ "NV",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )
unique(db$lifeForm)

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord)
db <- db %>% mutate(
    basisOfRecord = case_when(
      basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
      TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences empty

# bibliographicCitation empty


# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/AMAZ2024.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
db %>%
  mutate(matchCodeEpig = as.character(matchCodeEpig)) %>%
  count(matchCodeEpig, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

```{r check_all_fields}
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # it looks ok

# verbatimElevation
# empty in the raw original file

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty ok
glimpse(db$eventDate)
# si viene de numero
db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
# glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm)
db <- db %>% mutate(
    lifeForm = case_when(
      # lifeForm %in% c("Hemiepífita", "Hemiepifita") ~ "H",
      # lifeForm %in% c("Liana") ~ "NV",
      lifeForm %in% c("Epiphyte") ~ "E",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
unique(db$lifeForm)

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord)
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences empty

# bibliographicCitation empty


# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/bertelmann_h_2025_PETE.csv", na = "")
```

##### 15. Database SandoyaV

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/SandoyaV/sandoya_v_2007.csv\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[28/03/2026\] Check all fields by MJCH \[28/03/2026\] check_EpIG_taxo

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/SandoyaV/sandoya_v_2007.csv"
db <- read.csv(path)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) 
# author from backbone
# author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
#     AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
#   filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
#   distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# # fill scientificNameAuthorship
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
#   left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
#   mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
#       (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
#         !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
#       TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
#   select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     country = case_when(
#       country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
#       TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # it looks ok

# verbatimElevation
# empty in the raw original file

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty ok
glimpse(db$eventDate)
# si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
# glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
# db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm)
db <- db %>% mutate(
    lifeForm = case_when(
      lifeForm %in% c("Hemiepífita", "Hemiepifita") ~ "H",
      lifeForm %in% c("Liana") ~ "NV",
      lifeForm %in% c("Holoepiphyte") ~ "E",
      lifeForm %in% c("Facultative epiphyte") ~ "FAC",
      lifeForm %in% c("Accidental epiphyte") ~ "ACC",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
unique(db$lifeForm)

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord)
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences empty

# bibliographicCitation empty


# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/sandoya_v_2007.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_all_fields}
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/HGOM2025.csv", na = "")
```

##### 18. Database USD2025

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/USD2025/USD2025.csv\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[28/03/2026\] Check all fields by MJCH \[09/04/2026\] check_EpIG_taxo

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/USD2025/USD2025.csv"
db <- read.csv(path)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) 
# author from backbone
# author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
#     AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
#   filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
#   distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# # fill scientificNameAuthorship
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
#   left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
#   mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
#       (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
#         !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
#       TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
#   select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     country = case_when(
#       country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
#       TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # it looks ok

# verbatimElevation
# empty in the raw original file

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty ok
glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$eventDate)[1:30]
# si viene de numero
db$eventDate <- as.Date(as.numeric(db$eventDate), origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
# glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm)
db <- db %>% mutate(
    lifeForm = case_when(
      lifeForm %in% c("Hemiepífita", "Hemiepifita", "Hemiepiphyte") ~ "H",
      lifeForm %in% c("Liana") ~ "NV",
      lifeForm %in% c("Holoepiphyte", "Epiphyte") ~ "E",
      lifeForm %in% c("Facultative epiphyte") ~ "FAC",
      lifeForm %in% c("Accidental epiphyte") ~ "ACC",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
unique(db$lifeForm)

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord)
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen"
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences empty

# bibliographicCitation empty




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))




# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/USD2025.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_all_fields}
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) 
# author from backbone
# author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
#     AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
#   filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
#   distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# # fill scientificNameAuthorship
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
#   left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
#   mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
#       (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
#         !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
#       TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
#   select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     country = case_when(
#       country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
#       TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # it looks ok

# verbatimElevation
# empty in the raw original file

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty ok
glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$eventDate)[1:30]
# # si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(as.numeric(db$eventDate), origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
# glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
# db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm)
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     lifeForm = case_when(
#       lifeForm %in% c("Hemiepífita", "Hemiepifita", "Hemiepiphyte") ~ "H",
#       lifeForm %in% c("Liana") ~ "NV",
#       lifeForm %in% c("Holoepiphyte", "Epiphyte") ~ "E",
#       lifeForm %in% c("Facultative epiphyte") ~ "FAC",
#       lifeForm %in% c("Accidental epiphyte") ~ "ACC",
#       TRUE ~ NA_character_))
# unique(db$lifeForm)

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord)
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen"
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences empty

# bibliographicCitation empty


# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/BA2025.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```
