```{r check_all_fields}
# author from backbone
author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
    sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
    AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship
db <- db %>% mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    country_std = case_when(
      str_to_lower(country) %in% c("argentina") ~ "Argentina",
      str_to_lower(country) %in% c("brazil", "brasil") ~ "Brazil",
      str_to_lower(country) %in% c("colombia") ~ "Colombia",
      str_to_lower(country) %in% c("usa", "united states", "united states of america") ~ "United States",
      str_to_lower(country) %in% c("ecuador") ~ "Ecuador",
      str_to_lower(country) %in% c("méxico", "mexico") ~ "Mexico",
      str_to_lower(country) %in% c("costa rica") ~ "Costa Rica",
      str_to_lower(country) %in% c("peru", "perú") ~ "Peru",
      str_to_lower(country) %in% c("bolivia") ~ "Bolivia",
      str_to_lower(country) %in% c("guiana", "guyana") ~ "Guyana",
      TRUE ~ country),
    countryCode = case_when(
      country_std == "Argentina" ~ "AR",
      country_std == "Brazil" ~ "BR",
      country_std == "Colombia" ~ "CO",
      country_std == "United States" ~ "US",
      country_std == "Ecuador" ~ "EC",
      country_std == "Mexico" ~ "MX",
      country_std == "Costa Rica" ~ "CR",
      country_std == "Peru" ~ "PE",
      country_std == "Bolivia" ~ "BO",
      country_std == "Guyana" ~ "GY",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    country = country_std
  ) %>%
  select(-country_std)

# countryCode 
glimpse(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    stateProvince = coalesce(
      str_extract(locality, "(?i)(?<=Prov\\.\\s)[^:,]+"),
      str_extract(locality, "(?i)(?<=Depto\\.\\s)[^:,]+")
    ) %>%
    str_squish()
  )
table(db$stateProvince, useNA = "ifany")

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # created with verbatimElevation

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    verbatimElevation = str_extract(locality, "\\b\\d{2,5}(?:[.,]\\d+)?\\s*m\\s*s\\.?n\\.?m\\.?\\b|\\b\\d{2,5}(?:[.,]\\d+)?\\s*m\\b"),
    minimumElevationInMeters = verbatimElevation %>%
      str_extract("\\d{2,5}(?:[.,]\\d+)?") %>%
      str_replace(",", ".") %>%
      as.numeric())

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty 
class(db$eventDate) # as character
glimpse(db$eventDate)
db <- db %>%
  mutate(date_from_locality = str_extract(locality, "\\b\\d{1,2}-[IVXLCDM]{1,4}-\\d{4}\\b"),
    eventDate = case_when(
      is.na(date_from_locality) ~ NA_character_,
      TRUE ~ {
        x <- str_match(date_from_locality, "^(\\d{1,2})-([IVXLCDM]{1,4})-(\\d{4})$")
        day <- str_pad(x[, 2], 2, pad = "0")
        mon_roman <- x[, 3]
        year <- x[, 4]

        mon <- case_when(
          mon_roman == "I" ~ "01",
          mon_roman == "II" ~ "02",
          mon_roman == "III" ~ "03",
          mon_roman == "IV" ~ "04",
          mon_roman == "V" ~ "05",
          mon_roman == "VI" ~ "06",
          mon_roman == "VII" ~ "07",
          mon_roman == "VIII" ~ "08",
          mon_roman == "IX" ~ "09",
          mon_roman == "X" ~ "10",
          mon_roman == "XI" ~ "11",
          mon_roman == "XII" ~ "12",
          TRUE ~ NA_character_
        )

        ifelse(is.na(mon), NA_character_, paste(year, mon, day, sep = "-"))
      }
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-date_from_locality)
# glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
db <- db %>% mutate(year = as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)))
hist(db$year) # ok

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # standarize
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    lifeForm = as.character(lifeForm),
    lifeForm = na_if(str_squish(lifeForm), ""),
    lifeForm = case_when(
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("hemi[ -]?ep[ií]f|hemiepiph", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "H",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("facultative epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "FAC",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("accidental epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "ACC",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("ep[ií]fita|ep[ií]fito|epiphyte|epiphytic|epiphiyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "E",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("liana|enredadera|climber", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "NV",
      TRUE ~ lifeForm
    )
  )
unique(db$lifeForm) # ok

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
# db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen" 
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/literature2024.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
db %>%
  mutate(matchCodeEpig = as.character(matchCodeEpig)) %>%
  count(matchCodeEpig, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

```{r check_all_fields}
# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
db %>%
  mutate(
    lat_num = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    lon_num = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude)),
    has_coords = !is.na(lat_num) & !is.na(lon_num),
    zero_zero = has_coords & lat_num == 0 & lon_num == 0,
    lat_out = has_coords & (lat_num < -90 | lat_num > 90),
    lon_out = has_coords & (lon_num < -180 | lon_num > 180)
  ) %>%
  {
    x <- .
    list(
      resumen = tibble(
        total = nrow(x),
        con_coordenadas = sum(x$has_coords),
        sin_coordenadas = sum(!x$has_coords),
        zero_zero = sum(x$zero_zero),
        lat_fuera_rango = sum(x$lat_out),
        lon_fuera_rango = sum(x$lon_out)
      ),
      ejemplos_problematicos = x %>%
        filter(zero_zero | lat_out | lon_out) %>%
        select(country, stateProvince, locality, decimalLatitude, decimalLongitude) %>%
        head(30)
    )
  }
```

```{r check_all_fields}
# scientificNameAuthorship 
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) 
# author from backbone
author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
    sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
    AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship
db <- db %>% mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone

table(db$stateProvince, useNA = "ifany")

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
db %>%
  mutate(
    lat_num = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    lon_num = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude)),
    has_coords = !is.na(lat_num) & !is.na(lon_num),
    zero_zero = has_coords & lat_num == 0 & lon_num == 0,
    lat_out = has_coords & (lat_num < -90 | lat_num > 90),
    lon_out = has_coords & (lon_num < -180 | lon_num > 180)
  ) %>%
  {
    x <- .
    list(
      resumen = tibble(
        total = nrow(x),
        con_coordenadas = sum(x$has_coords),
        sin_coordenadas = sum(!x$has_coords),
        zero_zero = sum(x$zero_zero),
        lat_fuera_rango = sum(x$lat_out),
        lon_fuera_rango = sum(x$lon_out)
      ),
      ejemplos_problematicos = x %>%
        filter(zero_zero | lat_out | lon_out) %>%
        select(country, stateProvince, locality, decimalLatitude, decimalLongitude) %>%
        head(30)
    )
  }
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
glimpse(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # empty
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    minimumElevationInMeters = as.numeric(verbatimElevation),

  ) # created

#       str_extract("\\d{2,5}(?:[.,]\\d+)?") %>%
#       str_replace(",", ".") %>%
#       as.numeric())

db <- db %>% mutate(
```

```{r check_all_fields}
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%

unique(db$country)

unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
# create coordinates for match == 1
db %>%
  mutate(
    match_code = match_epig$match_code,
    has_coords = !is.na(decimalLatitude) & !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    locality_clean = stringr::str_to_lower(stringr::str_squish(locality)),
    geo_easy = stringr::str_detect(locality_clean, "\\d+\\s*km.*(de|from)")
  ) %>%
  filter(match_code %in% c("1", "2"), !has_coords) %>%
  summarise(
    total_sin_coords = n(),
    candidatos_faciles = sum(geo_easy)
  )
db_geo <- db %>% mutate(
    match_code = match_epig$match_code,
    has_coords = !is.na(decimalLatitude) & !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    locality_clean = stringr::str_to_lower(stringr::str_squish(locality))
  ) %>%
  filter(match_code %in% c("1", "2"), !has_coords) %>%
  mutate(
    distance_km = as.numeric(stringr::str_extract(locality_clean, "\\d+(?=\\s*km)")),
    place_ref = stringr::str_trim(
      stringr::str_replace(locality_clean, ".*?\\bkm\\s*(de|from)\\s+", "")
    ))
head(db_geo %>% select(locality, distance_km, place_ref), 20)
db_geo <- db %>%
  mutate(
    match_code = match_epig$match_code,
    has_coords = !is.na(decimalLatitude) & !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    locality_clean = stringr::str_to_lower(stringr::str_squish(locality))
  ) %>%
  filter(match_code %in% c("1", "2"), !has_coords) %>%
  mutate(
    distance_km = readr::parse_number(
      stringr::str_extract(locality_clean, "\\d+(?:[\\.,]\\d+)?\\s*km")
    ),
    place_ref = stringr::str_trim(
      stringr::str_replace(locality_clean, ".*?\\bde\\s+", "")
    )
  )
db_geo %>%
  select(locality, distance_km, place_ref) %>%
  head(30)
db_geo_ready <- db_geo %>%
  filter(!is.na(distance_km), !is.na(place_ref), place_ref != "") %>%
  distinct(place_ref, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    georef_status = "pending_geocoding",
    georef_method = "parsed_from_locality_km_reference",
    georef_notes = paste0("distance_km=", distance_km, "; place_ref=", place_ref)
  )
db <- db %>%
  left_join(
    db_geo %>%
      transmute(
        .row_id,
        distance_km,
        place_ref
      ),
    by = ".row_id"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0(
        "Parsed georeferencing candidates from locality into distance_km and place_ref; pending external geocoding [",
        Sys.Date(),
        "]"
      )))
nrow(db_geo_ready)
head(db_geo_ready %>% select(place_ref, distance_km, locality), 30)
db <- db %>%
  left_join(db_geo_ready %>%
      transmute(.row_id, distance_km, place_ref),
    by = ".row_id") %>%
  mutate(changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Prepared 463 georeferencing candidates from locality by extracting distance_km and cleaned place_ref for external geocoding [",
        Sys.Date(),
        "]")))
# library(tidygeocoder)
# actual georeferencing:
geo_base <- db_geo_ready %>%
  distinct(place_ref) %>%
  geocode(
    address = place_ref,
    method = "osm",
    lat = lat_ref,
    long = lon_ref)
# append changes
db_geo_ready <- db_geo_ready %>%
  left_join(geo_base, by = "place_ref")
db_geo_ready <- db_geo_ready %>%
  mutate(
    lat_ref = dplyr::coalesce(lat_ref.x, lat_ref.y),
    lon_ref = dplyr::coalesce(lon_ref.x, lon_ref.y)
  ) %>%
  select(-lat_ref.x, -lon_ref.x, -lat_ref.y, -lon_ref.y)
db <- db %>%left_join(
    db_geo_ready %>%
      select(.row_id, lat_ref, lon_ref),
    by = ".row_id") %>%
  mutate(decimalLatitude = if_else(is.na(decimalLatitude), lat_ref, decimalLatitude),
    decimalLongitude = if_else(is.na(decimalLongitude), lon_ref, decimalLongitude),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude from geocoded place_ref base locality using OSM via tidygeocoder [",
        Sys.Date(),"]"
      ))) %>%
  select(-lat_ref, -lon_ref)
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # ok
# check
db |>
  dplyr::filter(minimumElevationInMeters > 5000) |>
  dplyr::select(verbatimElevation, minimumElevationInMeters) |>
  head(50) # previous parsing wrong
# adjusting new parsing
db <- db |>
  mutate(
    verbatimElevation_clean = verbatimElevation,
    verbatimElevation_clean = str_replace_all(verbatimElevation_clean, "â€“|–|—", "-"),
    verbatimElevation_clean = str_replace_all(verbatimElevation_clean, "\\s+", " "),
    elev_numbers = str_extract_all(verbatimElevation_clean, "\\d+"),
    min_from_verbatim = sapply(elev_numbers, function(x) {
      if (length(x) >= 1) as.numeric(x[1]) else NA_real_
    }),
    max_from_verbatim = sapply(elev_numbers, function(x) {
      if (length(x) >= 2) as.numeric(x[2]) else NA_real_
    })
  )
db <- db |>
  mutate(
    minimumElevationInMeters = if_else(
      minimumElevationInMeters > 5000 & !is.na(min_from_verbatim),
      min_from_verbatim,
      minimumElevationInMeters))

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty 
class(db$eventDate) # empty
glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$verbatimEventDate)[1:30] # diferent formats
# create with verbatim

# si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(as.numeric(db$eventDate), origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
# glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# used to create date
meses <- c("enero" = "01","febrero" = "02",
  "marzo" = "03","abril" = "04",
  "mayo" = "05","junio" = "06",
  "julio" = "07","agosto" = "08",
  "septiembre" = "09","setiembre" = "09",
  "octubre" = "10","noviembre" = "11","diciembre" = "12")
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    eventDate = as.character(eventDate),
    verbatimEventDate = as.character(verbatimEventDate),
    eventDate = na_if(eventDate, ""),
    verbatimEventDate = na_if(verbatimEventDate, ""),
    verbatimEventDate_clean = str_to_lower(str_trim(verbatimEventDate)),
    valid_verbatim_date = grepl("^\\d{1,2}/[[:alpha:]áéíóú]+/\\d{4}$", verbatimEventDate_clean),
    day = if_else(valid_verbatim_date, str_extract(verbatimEventDate_clean, "^\\d{1,2}"), NA_character_),
    year = if_else(valid_verbatim_date, str_extract(verbatimEventDate_clean, "\\d{4}$"), NA_character_),
    month_text = if_else(
      valid_verbatim_date,
      str_extract(verbatimEventDate_clean, "(?<=/)[[:alpha:]áéíóú]+(?=/)"),
      NA_character_
    ),
    month = unname(meses[month_text]),
    eventDate_parsed = if_else(
      !is.na(day) & !is.na(month) & !is.na(year),
      paste(year, month, str_pad(day, 2, pad = "0"), sep = "-"),
      NA_character_
    ),
    eventDate = coalesce(eventDate, eventDate_parsed)
  ) %>%
  select(-verbatimEventDate_clean, -valid_verbatim_date, -day, -year, -month_text, -month, -eventDate_parsed)


# year
glimpse(db$year)
db$year <- as.integer(substr(db$eventDate, 1, 4))
hist(db$year) # ok

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # can standarized
# check classes
db <- db |>  mutate(lifeForm_from_habitat = case_when(
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("epiphyte|epifit|epífito", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "epiphyte",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("terrestrial|terrestre", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "terrestrial",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("rupicol|rock", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "rupicolous",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
table(db$lifeForm_from_habitat, useNA = "always")
# cases
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    lifeForm_from_habitat = case_when(
      str_detect(habitat, regex("epiphyte|epifit|epífito", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("facultative epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "facultative epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("accidental epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "accidental epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("hemiepiphyte|hemiepifit|hemiepífita", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Hemiepiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("liana", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Liana",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("lithophyte|rupicol|rock", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "rupicolous",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )
# lifeForm
db <- db %>% mutate(
    lifeForm = as.character(lifeForm),
    lifeForm = na_if(lifeForm, ""),
    lifeForm_raw = coalesce(lifeForm, lifeForm_from_habitat),
    lifeForm_raw = str_to_lower(str_squish(lifeForm_raw)),
    lifeForm = case_when(
      # explicit epiphyte categories first
      str_detect(lifeForm_raw, "facultative epiphyte") ~ "FAC",
      str_detect(lifeForm_raw, "accidental epiphyte") ~ "ACC",
      
      # prioritize epiphyte whenever mixed with other habits
      str_detect(lifeForm_raw, "epífita|epifita|epífito|epifito|epiphyte|holoepiphyte") ~ "E",
      
      # all climbing / nomadic vine forms
      str_detect(lifeForm_raw, "hemiepífita|hemiepifita|hemiepiphyte|liana|lianas|enredadera|trepadora|trepadoras|bejuco|escandente") ~ "NV",
      
      TRUE ~ lifeForm_raw
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-lifeForm_raw)
unique(db$lifeForm) # ok

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen" # added
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok



# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/OAX2025", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
  mutate(matchCodeEpig = as.character(matchCodeEpig)) %>%
  count(matchCodeEpig, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
```

##### 28. Database CR2024

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/CR2024/CR2024.csv\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[08/04/2026\] Check all fields by MJCH \[14/04/2026\] check_EpIG_taxo

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/CR2024/CR2024.csv"
db <- read.csv(path)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship 
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) 
# author from backbone
author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
    sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
    AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship
db <- db %>% mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    country = case_when(
      country == "México" ~ "Mexico",
      country == "Perú" ~ "Peru",
      country == "Panamá" ~ "Panama",
      country == "Estados Unidos" ~ "United States",
      country == "Belice" ~ "Belize",
      country == "Etiopia" ~ "Ethiopia",
      country == "Kenia" ~ "Kenya",
      country == "Santa Lucia" ~ "Saint Lucia",
      TRUE ~ country
    ),
    countryCode = case_when(
      country == "Costa Rica" ~ "CR",
      country == "Honduras" ~ "HN",
      country == "Panama" ~ "PA",
      country == "Belize" ~ "BZ",
      country == "Nicaragua" ~ "NI",
      country == "Guatemala" ~ "GT",
      country == "Mexico" ~ "MX",
      country == "Argentina" ~ "AR",
      country == "Peru" ~ "PE",
      country == "Colombia" ~ "CO",
      country == "El Salvador" ~ "SV",
      country == "United States" ~ "US",
      country == "Ecuador" ~ "EC",
      country == "Ethiopia" ~ "ET",
      country == "Kenya" ~ "KE",
      country == "Saint Lucia" ~ "LC",
      country == "Cuba" ~ "CU",
      country == "Uruguay" ~ "UY",
      TRUE ~ countryCode
    )
  )

# countryCode 
glimpse(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    stateProvince = coalesce(
      str_extract(locality, regex("^(?:prov\\.?\\s*(?:de\\s+)?)\\s*([^\\.:,]+)", ignore_case = TRUE)) %>%
        str_remove(regex("^(?:prov\\.?\\s*(?:de\\s+)?)", ignore_case = TRUE)),
      str_extract(locality, regex("^(?:depto\\.?\\s*(?:de\\s+)?)\\s*([^\\.:,]+)", ignore_case = TRUE)) %>%
        str_remove(regex("^(?:depto\\.?\\s*(?:de\\s+)?)", ignore_case = TRUE))
    ) %>%
      str_squish()
  )
table(db$stateProvince, useNA = "ifany")

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
db %>%
  mutate(
    lat_num = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    lon_num = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude)),
    has_coords = !is.na(lat_num) & !is.na(lon_num),
    zero_zero = has_coords & lat_num == 0 & lon_num == 0,
    lat_out = has_coords & (lat_num < -90 | lat_num > 90),
    lon_out = has_coords & (lon_num < -180 | lon_num > 180)
  ) %>%
  {
    x <- .
    list(
      resumen = tibble(
        total = nrow(x),
        con_coordenadas = sum(x$has_coords),
        sin_coordenadas = sum(!x$has_coords),
        zero_zero = sum(x$zero_zero),
        lat_fuera_rango = sum(x$lat_out),
        lon_fuera_rango = sum(x$lon_out)
      ),
      ejemplos_problematicos = x %>%
        filter(zero_zero | lat_out | lon_out) %>%
        select(country, stateProvince, locality, decimalLatitude, decimalLongitude) %>%
        head(30)
    )
  }
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
glimpse(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # non existent
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    verbatimElevation = str_extract(locality,
      regex("\\b\\d{2,5}(?:[.,]\\d+)?\\s*m\\s*(?:s\\.?n\\.?m\\.?|asl|elev\\.?|elevation)", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    minimumElevationInMeters = verbatimElevation %>%
      str_extract("\\d{2,5}(?:[.,]\\d+)?") %>%
      str_replace(",", ".") %>%
      as.numeric())
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # created with verbatimElevation

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)
# db <- db %>%
#   mutate(
#     verbatimElevation = str_extract(locality, "\\b\\d{2,5}(?:[.,]\\d+)?\\s*m\\s*s\\.?n\\.?m\\.?\\b|\\b\\d{2,5}(?:[.,]\\d+)?\\s*m\\b"),
#     minimumElevationInMeters = verbatimElevation %>%
#       str_extract("\\d{2,5}(?:[.,]\\d+)?") %>%
#       str_replace(",", ".") %>%
#       as.numeric())

# Dates -------------

# eventDate 
class(db$eventDate) # as character
glimpse(db$eventDate)
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    eventDate = dmy(eventDate),
    year = year(eventDate)
  )

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
# db <- db %>% mutate(year = as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)))
hist(db$year) # ok

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # standarize
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    lifeForm = as.character(lifeForm),
    lifeForm = na_if(str_squish(lifeForm), ""),
    lifeForm = case_when(
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("hemi[ -]?ep[ií]f|hemiepiph", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "H",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("facultative epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "FAC",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("accidental epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "ACC",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("ep[ií]fita|ep[ií]fito|epiphyte|epiphytic|epiphiyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "E",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("liana|enredadera|climber", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "NV",
      TRUE ~ lifeForm
    )
  )
unique(db$lifeForm) # ok

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen"
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------

db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>

# final filter -------------------

  db,
```

##### 29. Database LP2024

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/LP2024/LP2024.csv\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[08/04/2026\] Check all fields by MJCH \[14/04/2026\] check_EpIG_taxo

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/LP2024/LP2024.csv"
db <- read.csv(path)

# remove second row
db <- db %>% slice(-1)
names(db)

# change names to DwC
db <- db %>%
  rename(
    datasetID = `ID del conjunto de datos`,
    gbifID = `ID de GBIF`,
    occurrenceID = `ID de registro`,
    family = familia,
    scientificName = `Nombre de la especie`,
    taxonRank = `rango de taxón`,
    scientificName_std = nombrecientífico,
    verbatimScientificName = `NombreCientífico verbatim`,
    scientificNameAuthorship = `Nombre científico Autoría`,
    lifeForm = `Forma de vida`,
    individualCount = `recuento individual`,
    organismQuantity = organismo_Cantidad,
    organismQuantityType = organismo_Cantidad_Tipo,
    countryCode = `Código de país`,
    locality = localidad,
    decimalLatitude = `latitud decimal`,
    decimalLongitude = `longitud decimal`,
    verbatimElevation = Elevación,
    eventDate = `Fecha del evento`,
    basisOfRecord = baseDeRegistro,
    type = tipo,
    associatedReferences = `Referencias asociadas`,
    institutionCode = `Código de institución`,
    collectionCode = ColeccionCodigo,
    catalogNumber = `Número de catálogo`,
    recordedBy = `Registrado por`,
    recordNumber = `Número de registro`,
    identifiedBy = `identificado por`,
    occurrenceRemarks = Observaciones
  )
glimpse(db)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName 
db <- db %>% mutate(
    verbatimScientificName = coalesce(verbatimScientificName, scientificName),
    scientificName = if_else(
      str_detect(scientificName, regex("\\svar\\.?\\s", ignore_case = TRUE)),
      str_extract(scientificName, "^[A-Z][a-z-]+\\s+[a-z-]+"),
      scientificName
    ),
    taxonRank = case_when(
      str_detect(verbatimScientificName, regex("\\svar\\.?\\s", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "VARIETY",TRUE ~ taxonRank))
match_res <- match_species_backbone(
  spp = db,
  backbone = backbone$backbone,
  spp_col = "scientificName")
match_res <- attach_accepted_name(match_res, backbone$backbone)

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
  TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
)
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))
# family, genus, order
db <- fill_taxonomy(db, genus_lookup, family_lookup)
# fill for ferns
db <- db %>% mutate(
         order = case_when(
             family %in% c("Dryopteridaceae", "Oleandraceae") ~ "Polypodiales",
             TRUE ~ order))

# scientificNameAuthorship 
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) 
# author from backbone
author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
    sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
    AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    changes = if (!"changes" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(changes),
    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)
  ) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship
    ),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]")
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone

# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
db$country <- NA
# to English
db <- db %>%
  mutate(country = case_when(
      countryCode == "CO" ~ "Colombia",
      countryCode == "AR" ~ "Argentina",
      countryCode == "BO" ~ "Bolivia",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled country from countryCode (ISO2) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# countryCode 
glimpse(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty
db %>%
  filter(!is.na(locality), str_squish(locality) != "") %>%
  select(locality) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  head(40)
table(db$stateProvince, useNA = "ifany") # not possible to recover

# locality empty
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
db %>%
  mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude)),
    has_coords = !is.na(decimalLatitude) & !is.na(decimalLongitude)
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    con_coordenadas = sum(has_coords),
    sin_coordenadas = sum(!has_coords))
# NO COORDINATES --- I GEORREFERENCED 23
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters empty

# verbatimElevation empty

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty

# verbatimEventDate empty

# year empty


# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # standarize
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    lifeForm = as.character(lifeForm),
    lifeForm = na_if(str_squish(lifeForm), ""),
    lifeForm = case_when(
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("hemi[ -]?ep[iíì]f|hemiepiph", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "H",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("facultative epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "FAC",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("accidental epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "ACC",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("trunk epiphyte|ep[iíì]fita|ep[iíì]fito|epiphyte|epiphytic|epiphite|epiphy\\b|epifita", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "E",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("liana|enredadera|climber", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "NV",
      TRUE ~ lifeForm))
unique(db$lifeForm) # ok

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen"
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/LP2025.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
  mutate(matchCodeEpig = as.character(matchCodeEpig)) %>%
  count(matchCodeEpig, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

```{r check_all_fields}
# countryCode 
glimpse(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    stateProvince = coalesce(
      str_extract(locality, regex("^(?:prov\\.?\\s*(?:de\\s+)?)\\s*([^\\.:,]+)", ignore_case = TRUE)) %>%
        str_remove(regex("^(?:prov\\.?\\s*(?:de\\s+)?)", ignore_case = TRUE)),
      str_extract(locality, regex("^(?:depto\\.?\\s*(?:de\\s+)?)\\s*([^\\.:,]+)", ignore_case = TRUE)) %>%
        str_remove(regex("^(?:depto\\.?\\s*(?:de\\s+)?)", ignore_case = TRUE))
    ) %>%
      str_squish()
  )
table(db$stateProvince, useNA = "ifany")

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
db %>%
  mutate(
    lat_num = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    lon_num = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude)),
    has_coords = !is.na(lat_num) & !is.na(lon_num),
    zero_zero = has_coords & lat_num == 0 & lon_num == 0,
    lat_out = has_coords & (lat_num < -90 | lat_num > 90),
    lon_out = has_coords & (lon_num < -180 | lon_num > 180)
  ) %>%
  {
    x <- .
    list(
      resumen = tibble(
        total = nrow(x),
        con_coordenadas = sum(x$has_coords),
        sin_coordenadas = sum(!x$has_coords),
        zero_zero = sum(x$zero_zero),
        lat_fuera_rango = sum(x$lat_out),
        lon_fuera_rango = sum(x$lon_out)
      ),
      ejemplos_problematicos = x %>%
        filter(zero_zero | lat_out | lon_out) %>%
        select(country, stateProvince, locality, decimalLatitude, decimalLongitude) %>%
        head(30)
    )
  }
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
glimpse(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # non existent
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    minimumElevationInMeters = as.numeric(verbatimElevation),
    verbatimElevation = as.character(verbatimElevation)
  )
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # created with verbatimElevation

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)

# Dates -------------

# eventDate 
class(db$eventDate) # as character
# create
glimpse(db$eventDate)
db <- db %>% mutate(
    eventDate = as.Date(eventDate, origin = "1899-12-30"),
    year = as.integer(format(eventDate, "%Y")))
db %>% select(eventDate, year) %>% head()

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
# db <- db %>% mutate(year = as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)))
hist(db$year) # ok

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # empty
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    lifeForm = NA_character_,
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("No lifeForm information available in dataset; field created as NA [", Sys.Date(), "]")
    )
  )

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
# db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen"
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)
```

```{r check_all_fields}
#       lifeForm_raw %in% c("Liana") ~ "NV", # asuming nomadic vine
#       lifeForm_raw %in% c("Holoepiphyte", "Epiphyte", "epiphyte") ~ "E",
#       lifeForm_raw %in% c("Facultative epiphyte", "facultative epiphyte") ~ "FAC",
#       lifeForm_raw %in% c("Accidental epiphyte", "accidental epiphyte") ~ "ACC",
#       TRUE ~ lifeForm_raw
#     )
#   ) %>%
#   select(-lifeForm_raw)
unique(db$lifeForm) # ok

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen" # added
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/SURCO2025.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
db %>%
  mutate(matchCodeEpig = as.character(matchCodeEpig)) %>%
  count(matchCodeEpig, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

##### 32. Database CAUP2023

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/CAUP2023/CAUP2023.csv\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[08/04/2026\] Check all fields by MJCH \[14/04/2026\] check_EpIG_taxo

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/CAUP2023/CAUP2023.csv"
db <- read.csv(path)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
  TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
)
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship 
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
# class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) 
# # author from backbone
# author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
#     AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
#   filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
#   distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# # fill scientificNameAuthorship
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
#   left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
#   mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
#       (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
#         !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
#       TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
#   select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     country = case_when(
#       country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
#       TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE) # empty
# # create
# db <- db %>%
#   mutate(
#     minimumElevationInMeters = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(verbatimElevation)),
#     verbatimElevation = as.character(verbatimElevation)
#   )
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # weird

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)

# Dates -------------

glimpse(db$eventDate)
```

```{r check_all_fields}
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet

# 7. #save changes

      str_detect(eventDate_raw, "^00/\\d{2}/\\d{4}$") ~ str_replace(eventDate_raw, "^00/(\\d{2})/(\\d{4})$", "\\2-\\1"),
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    year = case_when(
      str_detect(eventDate, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$") ~ as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)),
      str_detect(eventDate, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}$") ~ as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)),
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-eventDate_raw)

### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/BAR2025.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/GerberJP/gerber_jp_2025.csv"
db <- read.csv(path)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship 
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
# class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) 
# # author from backbone
# author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
#     AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
#   filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
#   distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# # fill scientificNameAuthorship
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
#   left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
#   mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
#       (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
#         !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
#       TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
#   select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     country = case_when(
#       country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
#       TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE) # empty
# create
# db <- db %>%
#   mutate(
#     minimumElevationInMeters = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(verbatimElevation)),
#     verbatimElevation = as.character(verbatimElevation)
#   )
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # weird

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)
# used to correct the previous

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty 
class(db$eventDate) # as character
glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$eventDate)[1:30] # some errors

unique(db$lifeForm) # can be extracted from habitat
```

```{r check_all_fields}
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:

# eventDate empty

glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$eventDate)[1:30] # some errors

    eventDate = as.character(as.Date(eventDate)),
    year = as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)))

# lifeForm

# check classes
db <- db |>  mutate(lifeForm_from_habitat = case_when(
      str_detect(habitat, regex("epiphyte|epifit|epífito", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("terrestrial|terrestre", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "terrestrial",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("rupicol|rock", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "rupicolous",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
table(db$lifeForm_from_habitat, useNA = "always")
# cases
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    lifeForm_from_habitat = case_when(
      str_detect(habitat, regex("epiphyte|epifit|epífito", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("facultative epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "facultative epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("accidental epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "accidental epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("hemiepiphyte|hemiepifit|hemiepífita", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Hemiepiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("liana", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Liana",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("lithophyte|rupicol|rock", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "rupicolous",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )
# lifeForm
db <- db %>%
  mutate(lifeForm = as.character(lifeForm),
    lifeForm = na_if(lifeForm, ""),
    lifeForm_raw = coalesce(lifeForm, lifeForm_from_habitat),
    lifeForm = case_when(
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Hemiepífita", "Hemiepifita", "Hemiepiphyte") ~ "H",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Liana") ~ "NV", # asuming nomadic vine
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Holoepiphyte", "Epiphyte", "epiphyte") ~ "E",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Facultative epiphyte", "facultative epiphyte") ~ "FAC",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Accidental epiphyte", "accidental epiphyte") ~ "ACC",
      TRUE ~ lifeForm_raw
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-lifeForm_raw)
unique(db$lifeForm) # ok

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen" # added

unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------

    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/CPUN2025.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_all_fields}
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship 
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
# author from backbone
author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
    sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
    AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship
db <- db %>% mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     country = case_when(
#       country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
#       TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # ok

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE) # empty
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # ok

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty 
class(db$eventDate) # as character
glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$eventDate)[1:30] # some errors
db <- db %>%mutate(
    eventDate = as.character(as.Date(eventDate)),
    year = as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)))
# si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(as.numeric(db$eventDate), origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
# db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))
hist(db$year) # ok

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # can be extracted from habitat
# check classes
db <- db |>  mutate(lifeForm_from_habitat = case_when(
      str_detect(habitat, regex("epiphyte|epifit|epífito", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("terrestrial|terrestre", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "terrestrial",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("rupicol|rock", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "rupicolous",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
table(db$lifeForm_from_habitat, useNA = "always")
# cases
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    lifeForm_from_habitat = case_when(
      str_detect(habitat, regex("epiphyte|epifit|epífito", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("facultative epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "facultative epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("accidental epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "accidental epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("hemiepiphyte|hemiepifit|hemiepífita", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Hemiepiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("liana", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Liana",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("lithophyte|rupicol|rock", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "rupicolous",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )
# lifeForm
db <- db %>%
  mutate(lifeForm = as.character(lifeForm),
    lifeForm = na_if(lifeForm, ""),
    lifeForm_raw = coalesce(lifeForm, lifeForm_from_habitat),
    lifeForm = case_when(
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Hemiepífita", "Hemiepifita", "Hemiepiphyte") ~ "H",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Liana") ~ "NV", # asuming nomadic vine
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Holoepiphyte", "Epiphyte", "epiphyte") ~ "E",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Facultative epiphyte", "facultative epiphyte") ~ "FAC",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Accidental epiphyte", "accidental epiphyte") ~ "ACC",
      TRUE ~ lifeForm_raw
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-lifeForm_raw)
unique(db$lifeForm) # ok

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen" # added
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/ISV2025.csv", na = "")
```
