```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
  mutate(matchCodeEpig = as.character(matchCodeEpig)) %>%
  count(matchCodeEpig, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

```{r check_all_fields}
# taxonRank

  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
  stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
  TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
)

  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship

    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)

      TRUE ~ str_squish(str_to_lower(lifeForm))

  )
```

```{r check_all_fields}
    eventDate = as.character(as.Date(eventDate)),
    year = as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)))

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT

  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/ELE2025.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/EBUM2024/EBUM2024.csv"
db <- read.csv(path)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

unique(db$taxonRank)

#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(

# scientificNameAuthorship 
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)

#     sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
#     AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
#   filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
#   distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# # fill scientificNameAuthorship
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
#   left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
#   mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
#       (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
#         !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
#       TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
#   select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     country = case_when(
#       country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
#       TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE) # empty
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    verbatimElevation = na_if(str_squish(verbatimElevation), ""),
    minimumElevationInMeters = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(verbatimElevation))
  )
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # weird

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)
# used to correct the previous

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty 
class(db$eventDate) # as character
glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$eventDate)[1:30] # some errors
# db <- db %>%mutate(
#     eventDate = as.character(as.Date(eventDate)),
#     year = as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)))

# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
glimpse(db$eventDate)

hist(db$year) # ok

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # can not be deduced
db <- db %>% mutate(
    lifeForm = NA_character_,
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("No lifeForm information available or recoverable from text fields; lifeForm left as NA [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen" # added

#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",

# associatedReferences

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------

  db,
```

```{r check_all_fields}
# si viene de numero

# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
# glimpse(db$eventDate)
# if is text
meses <- c(
  "enero" = "01", "febrero" = "02", "marzo" = "03",
  "abril" = "04", "mayo" = "05", "junio" = "06",
  "julio" = "07", "agosto" = "08", "septiembre" = "09",
  "setiembre" = "09", "octubre" = "10",
  "noviembre" = "11", "diciembre" = "12"
)
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    verbatimEventDate_clean = stringr::str_to_lower(verbatimEventDate),
    verbatimEventDate_clean = stringr::str_trim(verbatimEventDate_clean),
    day = stringr::str_extract(verbatimEventDate_clean, "^\\d{1,2}"),
    year = stringr::str_extract(verbatimEventDate_clean, "\\d{4}$"),
    month_text = stringr::str_extract(
      verbatimEventDate_clean,
      paste(names(meses), collapse = "|")
    ),
    month = unname(meses[month_text]),
    eventDate = ifelse(
      !is.na(day) & !is.na(month) & !is.na(year),
      paste(year, month, stringr::str_pad(day, 2, pad = "0"), sep = "-"),
      NA
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-verbatimEventDate_clean, -day, -month_text, -month)
# check
table(is.na(db$eventDate)) # ok
head(db$eventDate) # ok
class(db$eventDate) # character
db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate) # now ok

# verbatimEventDate
# used to fill previous field

# year
glimpse(db$year)
db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))
hist(db$year) # ok

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # can be extracted from occurrenceRemarks
# check classes
db <- db |>  mutate(lifeForm_from_habitat = case_when(
      str_detect(occurrenceRemarks, regex("epiphyte|epifit|epífito|epiphy", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "epiphyte",
      str_detect(occurrenceRemarks, regex("terrestrial|terrestre", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "terrestrial",
      str_detect(occurrenceRemarks, regex("rupicol|rock|lythoph|litof", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "rupicolous",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
table(db$lifeForm_from_habitat, useNA = "always")
# cases
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    lifeForm_from_habitat = case_when(
      str_detect(occurrenceRemarks, regex("epiphyte|epifit|epífito", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "epiphyte",
      str_detect(occurrenceRemarks, regex("facultative epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "facultative epiphyte",
      str_detect(occurrenceRemarks, regex("accidental epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "accidental epiphyte",
      str_detect(occurrenceRemarks, regex("hemiepiphyte|hemiepifit|hemiepífita", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Hemiepiphyte",
      str_detect(occurrenceRemarks, regex("liana", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Liana",
      str_detect(occurrenceRemarks, regex("lithophyte|rupicol|rock", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "rupicolous",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )
# lifeForm
db <- db %>%
  mutate(lifeForm = as.character(lifeForm),
    lifeForm = na_if(lifeForm, ""),
    lifeForm_raw = coalesce(lifeForm, lifeForm_from_habitat),
    lifeForm = case_when(
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Hemiepífita", "Hemiepifita", "Hemiepiphyte") ~ "H",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Liana") ~ "NV", # asuming nomadic vine
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Holoepiphyte", "Epiphyte", "epiphyte") ~ "E",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Facultative epiphyte", "facultative epiphyte") ~ "FAC",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Accidental epiphyte", "accidental epiphyte") ~ "ACC",
      TRUE ~ lifeForm_raw
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-lifeForm_raw)
unique(db$lifeForm) # ok

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # empty
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen" # added
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok



### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/LP2024.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
db %>%
  mutate(matchCodeEpig = as.character(matchCodeEpig)) %>%
  count(matchCodeEpig, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

##### 41. Database JimenezDA – jimenez_d_2023_b_usumacinta

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/JimenezDA/jimenez_d_2023_b_usumacinta.csv\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[08/04/2026\] Check all fields by MJCH \[14/04/2026\] check_EpIG_taxo

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/JimenezDA/jimenez_d_2023_usumacinta.csv"
db <- read.csv(path)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship 
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
# author from backbone
author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
    sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
    AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship
db <- db %>% mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     country = case_when(
#       country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
#       TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE) # empty
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # weird

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)
# used to correct the previous

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty 
class(db$eventDate) # as character
glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$eventDate)[1:30] # some errors
# db <- db %>%mutate(
#     eventDate = as.character(as.Date(eventDate)),
#     year = as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)))
# si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(as.numeric(db$eventDate), origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
# db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))
hist(db$year) # ok

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # can be extracted from habitat
# check classes
db <- db |>  mutate(lifeForm_from_habitat = case_when(
      str_detect(habitat, regex("epiphyte|epifit|epífito", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("terrestrial|terrestre", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "terrestrial",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("rupicol|rock", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "rupicolous",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
table(db$lifeForm_from_habitat, useNA = "always")
# cases
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    lifeForm_from_habitat = case_when(
      str_detect(habitat, regex("epiphyte|epifit|epífito", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("facultative epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "facultative epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("accidental epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "accidental epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("hemiepiphyte|hemiepifit|hemiepífita", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Hemiepiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("liana", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Liana",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("lithophyte|rupicol|rock", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "rupicolous",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )
# lifeForm
db <- db %>%
  mutate(lifeForm = as.character(lifeForm),
    lifeForm = na_if(lifeForm, ""),
    lifeForm_raw = coalesce(lifeForm, lifeForm_from_habitat),
    lifeForm = case_when(
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Hemiepífita", "Hemiepifita", "Hemiepiphyte") ~ "H",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Liana") ~ "NV", # asuming nomadic vine
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Holoepiphyte", "Epiphyte", "epiphyte") ~ "E",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Facultative epiphyte", "facultative epiphyte") ~ "FAC",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Accidental epiphyte", "accidental epiphyte") ~ "ACC",
      TRUE ~ lifeForm_raw
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-lifeForm_raw)
unique(db$lifeForm) # ok

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen" # added
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)
```

##### 43. Database ZizkaA

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/ZizkaA/zizka_a_2019.csv\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[08/04/2026\] Check all fields by MJCH \[14/04/2026\] check_EpIG_taxo

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/ZizkaA/zizka_a_2019.csv"
db <- read.csv(path)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship 
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
# class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) 
# # author from backbone
# author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
#     AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
#   filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
#   distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# # fill scientificNameAuthorship
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
#   left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
#   mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
#       (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
#         !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
#       TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
#   select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     country = case_when(
#       country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
#       TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE) # empty
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # weird

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)
# used to correct the previous

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty 
class(db$eventDate) # as character
glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$eventDate)[1:30] # some errors
# db <- db %>%mutate(
#     eventDate = as.character(as.Date(eventDate)),
#     year = as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)))
# si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(as.numeric(db$eventDate), origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
# db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))
hist(db$year) # ok

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # can be extracted from habitat
# check classes

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
# db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen" # added
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/zizka_a_2019.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

```{r check_all_fields}
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet

    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters

# minimumElevationInMeters

# verbatimElevation
```

```{r check_all_fields}
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    minimumElevationInMeters = as.numeric(verbatimElevation),
    verbatimElevation = as.character(verbatimElevation)
  )
# used to correct the previous

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty 
class(db$eventDate) # as character
glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$eventDate)[1:30] # some errors
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    eventDate = str_squish(eventDate),
    eventDate = na_if(eventDate, ""),
    # detectar números (Excel)
    eventDate_num = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(eventDate)),
    # convertir
    eventDate = case_when(
      !is.na(eventDate_num) ~ as.Date(eventDate_num, origin = "1899-12-30"),
      str_detect(eventDate, "^\\d{2}/\\d{2}/\\d{4}$") ~ as.Date(eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y"),
      TRUE ~ NA_Date_)) %>%
  select(-eventDate_num)
# si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(as.numeric(db$eventDate), origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))
hist(db$year) # ok

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # can be extracted from habitat
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    lifeForm = str_squish(as.character(lifeForm)),
    lifeForm = na_if(lifeForm, ""),
    lifeForm = case_when(
      lifeForm %in% c("E", "e") ~ "E",
      lifeForm == "HE" ~ "H",
      TRUE ~ lifeForm))
unique(db$lifeForm) # ok

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen" # added
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/GUYN2008.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/HFCF2019/HFCF2019.csv"
db <- read.csv(path)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship 
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
# author from backbone
author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
    sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
    AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship
db <- db %>% mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE) # empty
db %>%
  filter(!is.na(minimumElevationInMeters), minimumElevationInMeters > 5000 | minimumElevationInMeters < 0) %>%
  select(scientificName, country, stateProvince, locality,
         minimumElevationInMeters, verbatimElevation) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  arrange(desc(minimumElevationInMeters))
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    minimumElevationInMeters = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(minimumElevationInMeters)),
    minimumElevationInMeters = case_when(
      minimumElevationInMeters < 0 ~ NA_real_,
      minimumElevationInMeters > 10000 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ minimumElevationInMeters
    ),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Set implausible minimumElevationInMeters values (>10000 m or <0) to NA [", Sys.Date(), "]")
    )
  )
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # weird

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)
```

```{r check_all_fields}
countries_sf <- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(
  scale = 10,
  returnclass = "sf"
) %>%
  st_make_valid() %>%
  st_transform(4326) %>%
  select(
    country_from_coords = admin,
    countryCode_from_coords = iso_a2,
    geometry
  )
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    .row_id = row_number(),
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))
  )
pts <- db %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90,
    decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180,
    decimalLongitude <= 180
  ) %>%
  st_as_sf(
    coords = c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude"),
    crs = 4326,
    remove = FALSE
  )
pts_country <- sf::st_join(
  pts,
  countries_sf,
  join = sf::st_within,
  left = TRUE
) %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(.row_id, country_from_coords, countryCode_from_coords)
db <- db %>%
  left_join(pts_country, by = ".row_id") %>%
  mutate(
    country = coalesce(country_from_coords),
    countryCode = coalesce(countryCode_from_coords)
  ) %>%
  select(-country_from_coords, -countryCode_from_coords)

hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # maximum is wrong
library(elevatr)
elevatr::get_elev_point(
  locations = data.frame(x = -79.51456, y = -6.149237),
  prj = "EPSG:4326",
  src = "aws") # 1408
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    minimumElevationInMeters = if_else(
      occurrenceID == "KUELAP2024_4522",
      1408,
      minimumElevationInMeters),
    changes = if_else(
      occurrenceID == "KUELAP2024_4522",
      append_change_tag(
        changes,

      ),
      changes))
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE) # looks better
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) #ok

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation) # some in verbatim and not in minimumElevationInMeters
db %>%
  filter(!is.na(verbatimElevation) & verbatimElevation != "") %>%
  count(verbatimElevation, sort = TRUE) %>%
  head(20)
# Parse simple elevations from verbatimElevation
# Accepted examples: "2040 m", "850msnm", "880 msnm", "245M"
# Rejected for now: empty strings, ranges such as "1700-1800"
parse_elevation_safe <- function(x) {
  x <- stringr::str_squish(as.character(x))
  x <- stringr::str_to_lower(x)
  # empty strings to NA
  x[x == ""] <- NA_character_
  # reject ranges
  is_range <- stringr::str_detect(x, "^\\d+\\s*[-–]\\s*\\d+")
  x[is_range] <- NA_character_
  # accept simple numeric elevations with optional units
  valid <- stringr::str_detect(
    x,
    "^\\d+\\s*(m|msnm)?$"
  )
  out <- ifelse(
    valid,
    as.numeric(stringr::str_extract(x, "\\d+")),
    NA_real_
  )
  out
}
db <- db %>% mutate(
    elevation_parsed = parse_elevation_safe(verbatimElevation))
# Quick QA
db %>% summarise(
    n_total = n(),
    n_verbatim_nonempty = sum(!is.na(verbatimElevation) & stringr::str_squish(verbatimElevation) != ""),
    n_parsed = sum(!is.na(elevation_parsed)),
    n_min_before = sum(!is.na(minimumElevationInMeters)),
    n_fillable = sum(is.na(minimumElevationInMeters) & !is.na(elevation_parsed)))
# Inspect the verbatim values that were not parsed
db %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(verbatimElevation),
    stringr::str_squish(verbatimElevation) != "",
    is.na(elevation_parsed)
  ) %>%
  count(verbatimElevation, sort = TRUE)
# Fill only missing minimumElevationInMeters
db <- db %>%
  mutate(minimumElevationInMeters = dplyr::coalesce(
      suppressWarnings(as.numeric(minimumElevationInMeters)),
      elevation_parsed),
    changes = if_else(
      is.na(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(minimumElevationInMeters))) & !is.na(elevation_parsed),
      append_change_tag(
        changes,
        paste0("minimumElevationInMeters filled from verbatimElevation [", Sys.Date(), "]")),
      changes))
# check again
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE) # looks better
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) #ok

# Dates -------------

# eventDate no date
db$eventDate <- NA
# glimpse(db$eventDate)
# unique(db$eventDate)[1:30]
# si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(as.numeric(db$eventDate), origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
# glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# non existing in original file

# year non existent, create in NA
db$year <- NA
# glimpse(db$year)
# db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm)
db <- db %>% mutate(
    lifeForm = case_when(
      lifeForm %in% c("Hemiepífita", "Hemiepifita", "Hemiepiphyte") ~ "H",
      lifeForm %in% c("Liana") ~ "NV",
      lifeForm %in% c("Holoepiphyte", "Epiphyte") ~ "E",
      lifeForm %in% c("Facultative epiphyte") ~ "FAC",
      lifeForm %in% c("Accidental epiphyte") ~ "ACC",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
unique(db$lifeForm)

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode non extant
db$institutionCode <- "KUELAP"

# collectionCode non extant
db$collectionCode <- NA

# recordedBy, recordNumber non extant
db$recordedBy <- NA
db$recordNumber <- NA

# catalogNumber ok
unique(db$catalogNumber) 

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord)
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen"
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT

  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/KUELAP2024.csv", na = "")
```

##### 49. Database TECLA2025

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/TECLA2025/TECLA2025.csv\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[08/04/2026\] Check all fields by MJCH \[16/04/2026\] check_EpIG_taxo

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/TECLA2025/TECLA2025.csv"
db <- read.csv(path)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship 
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
# author from backbone
author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
    sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
    AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship
db <- db %>% mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     country = case_when(
#       country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
#       TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE) # empty
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # weird

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)
# used to correct the previous

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty 
class(db$eventDate) # as character
glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$eventDate)[1:30] # some errors
db <- db %>%mutate(
    eventDate = as.character(as.Date(eventDate)),
    year = as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)))
# si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(as.numeric(db$eventDate), origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
# db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))
hist(db$year) # ok

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # can be extracted from habitat
unique(db$lifeForm) # ok

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
# db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen" # added
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/TECLA2025.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
db %>%
  mutate(matchCodeEpig = as.character(matchCodeEpig)) %>%
  count(matchCodeEpig, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

```{r check_all_fields}
  mutate(
    minimumElevationInMeters = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(minimumElevationInMeters)),
    minimumElevationInMeters = case_when(
      is.na(minimumElevationInMeters) ~ NA_real_,
      minimumElevationInMeters < 0 ~ NA_real_,
      minimumElevationInMeters > 6000 & minimumElevationInMeters <= 15000 ~ round(minimumElevationInMeters * 0.3048),
      minimumElevationInMeters > 15000 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ minimumElevationInMeters
    ),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Cleaned minimumElevationInMeters: negatives to NA, values 6001-15000 assumed feet and converted to meters, >15000 set to NA [", Sys.Date(), "]")
    )
  )

    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
saveRDS(db, file = path)

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/INABIO2025.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/HUA2025/HUA2025.RData"
load(path)
# db <- epi_taxo

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship) 
# author from backbone
# author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
#     AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
#   filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
#   distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# # fill scientificNameAuthorship
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
#     sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
#   left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
#   mutate(
#     scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
#       (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
#         !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
#       TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
#   select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     country = case_when(
#       country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
#       TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) # weird
# check
summary(db$minimumElevationInMeters)
sort(unique(db$minimumElevationInMeters), decreasing = TRUE)[1:100]
db |> 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(minimumElevationInMeters)) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(minimumElevationInMeters)) |> 
  dplyr::select(scientificName, verbatimElevation, minimumElevationInMeters, maximumElevationInMeters) |> 
  head(50)
# only four records wrong
db <- db |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    minimumElevationInMeters = dplyr::if_else(
      minimumElevationInMeters > maximumElevationInMeters &
      !is.na(maximumElevationInMeters),
      NA_real_,
      minimumElevationInMeters
    )
  )
# remove negatives
db <- db |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    minimumElevationInMeters = ifelse(minimumElevationInMeters < 0, NA, minimumElevationInMeters)
  )
# verification
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters)

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation) # not necessary since elevation is ok

# Dates -------------

# eventDate ok
class(db$eventDate)
glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$eventDate)[1:30]
# si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(as.numeric(db$eventDate), origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
# glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
unique(db$year)[1:30]
# extracting from date
db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))
unique(db$year)[1:30]
hist(db$year)

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm 
unique(db$lifeForm) # not included
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     lifeForm = case_when(
#       lifeForm %in% c("Hemiepífita", "Hemiepifita", "Hemiepiphyte") ~ "H",
#       lifeForm %in% c("Liana") ~ "NV",
#       lifeForm %in% c("Holoepiphyte", "Epiphyte") ~ "E",
#       lifeForm %in% c("Facultative epiphyte") ~ "FAC",
#       lifeForm %in% c("Accidental epiphyte") ~ "ACC",
#       TRUE ~ NA_character_))
# unique(db$lifeForm)

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber 
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# ok

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord)
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen"

#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) # empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation empty
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) # non existent



### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.)))))


# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
```

##### 53. Database ATRIUM2017

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/ATRIUM2017/ATRIUM2017.rds\]

Who & what: Exploration run by MJCH \[08/04/2026\] Check all fields by MJCH \[14/04/2026\] check_EpIG_taxo

```{r check_all_fields}
path <- "data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/ATRIUM2017/ATRIUM2017.rds"
db <- readRDS(path)

# Taxonomy -----------------

# scientificName was already checked and matches the Accepted name in the EpIG backbone only for records with matchCodeEpig == 1
# matchedNameEpig, matchedNameRawEpig, synonymEpig and matchCodeEpig were already added through the EpIG backbone workflow

# taxonRank
unique(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- stringr::str_trim(db$taxonRank)
# db$taxonRank <- dplyr::case_when(
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("especie") ~ "SPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("subsp.", "subsp") ~ "SUBSPECIES",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("var.", "var") ~ "VARIETY",
#   stringr::str_to_lower(db$taxonRank) %in% c("hibrido", "híbrido") ~ "SPECIES",
#   TRUE ~ db$taxonRank
# )
table(db$taxonRank, useNA = "ifany")

# kingdom, phylum empty and not required

# order, family ok
# db <- db %>% fill_taxonomy(
#     genus_lookup = genus_lookup,
#     family_lookup = family_lookup) %>%
#   mutate(
#     changes = append_change_tag(
#       changes,
#       paste0("Filled missing family/order/phylum from taxonomy lookups [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# scientificNameAuthorship 
head(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
class(db$scientificNameAuthorship)
# author from backbone
author_map <- backbone$backbone %>% transmute(
    sci_key = norm_name_key(ABBREVIAT),
    AUTHOR  = str_squish(as.character(AUTHOR))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sci_key), sci_key != "") %>%
  distinct(sci_key, .keep_all = TRUE)
# fill scientificNameAuthorship
db <- db %>% mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = if (!"scientificNameAuthorship" %in% names(.)) NA_character_ else as.character(scientificNameAuthorship),
    sci_key = norm_name_key(scientificName)) %>%
  left_join(author_map, by = "sci_key") %>%
  mutate(
    scientificNameAuthorship = case_when(
      (is.na(scientificNameAuthorship) | str_squish(scientificNameAuthorship) == "") &
        !is.na(AUTHOR) & str_squish(AUTHOR) != "" ~ AUTHOR,
      TRUE ~ scientificNameAuthorship),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Filled missing scientificNameAuthorship from EpIG backbone [", Sys.Date(), "]"))) %>%
  select(-sci_key, -AUTHOR)
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     country = case_when(
#       country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
#       TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE) 
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    minimumElevationInMeters = case_when(
      !is.na(minimumElevationInMeters) & minimumElevationInMeters > 5000 ~ round(minimumElevationInMeters * 0.3048),
      TRUE ~ minimumElevationInMeters
    ),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("Converted minimumElevationInMeters values >5000 from feet to meters [", Sys.Date(), "]")
    )
  )
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) 

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty 
class(db$eventDate) # as character
glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$eventDate)[1:30] # some errors
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    eventDate = str_squish(eventDate),
    eventDate = as.character(as.Date(eventDate)),
    year = as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)))
# si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(as.numeric(db$eventDate), origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
# db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))
hist(db$year) # ok

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # can be extracted from habitat
# check classes
db <- db |>  mutate(lifeForm_from_habitat = case_when(
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("epiphyte|epifit|epífito", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "epiphyte",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("terrestrial|terrestre", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "terrestrial",
      str_detect(lifeForm, regex("rupicol|rock", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "rupicolous",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_))
table(db$lifeForm_from_habitat, useNA = "always")
# cases
db <- db %>%
  mutate(
    lifeForm_from_habitat = case_when(
      str_detect(habitat, regex("epiphyte|epifit|epífito", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("facultative epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "facultative epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("accidental epiphyte", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "accidental epiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("hemiepiphyte|hemiepifit|hemiepífita", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Hemiepiphyte",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("liana", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Liana",
      str_detect(habitat, regex("lithophyte|rupicol|rock", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "rupicolous",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )
# lifeForm
db <- db %>%
  mutate(lifeForm = as.character(lifeForm),
    lifeForm = na_if(lifeForm, ""),
    lifeForm_raw = coalesce(lifeForm, lifeForm_from_habitat),
    lifeForm = case_when(
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Hemiepífita", "Hemiepifita", "Hemiepiphyte") ~ "H",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Liana") ~ "NV", # asuming nomadic vine
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Holoepiphyte", "Epiphyte", "epiphyte") ~ "E",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Facultative epiphyte", "facultative epiphyte") ~ "FAC",
      lifeForm_raw %in% c("Accidental epiphyte", "accidental epiphyte") ~ "ACC",
      TRUE ~ lifeForm_raw
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-lifeForm_raw)
unique(db$lifeForm) # ok

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen" # added
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
saveRDS(db, file = path)

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/ATRIUM2017.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
  count(matchCodeEpig, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)
```

```{r check_all_fields}
# has species + author since this is how it is created in the backbone


# Geography ------------

# country
unique(db$country)
# to English
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     country = case_when(
#       country %in% c("Perú", "Peru") ~ "Peru",
#       TRUE ~ country))

# countryCode 
unique(db$countryCode) #ok

# stateProvince
glimpse(db$stateProvince) # empty

# locality is present and useful
glimpse(db$locality)

# decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude ok
#clean coordinates
# 1. Preserve original row identity
db <- db %>% mutate(.row_id = row_number())
# 2. Keep only records with formally valid coordinates for CoordinateCleaner
db_cc <- db %>% mutate(
    decimalLatitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLatitude)),
    decimalLongitude = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(decimalLongitude))) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude >= -90, decimalLatitude <= 90,
    decimalLongitude >= -180, decimalLongitude <= 180)
# 3. Run CoordinateCleaner without sea test
cc_pass <- clean_coordinates(
  x = db_cc,
  lon = "decimalLongitude",
  lat = "decimalLatitude",
  species = "scientificName",
  tests = c("validity", "equal", "zeros", "capitals", "centroids", "gbif", "institutions"),
  value = "flagged")
# 4. Attach result back to the subset using the preserved row id
cc_tbl <- tibble(
  .row_id = db_cc$.row_id,
  coord_qc_pass = cc_pass)
# 5. Join back to the full original table
db <- db %>% left_join(cc_tbl, by = ".row_id")
# 6. Create .ok_for_analysis:
#    TRUE  = has coords and passes CC
#    FALSE = has coords and fails CC
#    NA    = no usable coords yet
db <- db %>%  mutate(
    .ok_for_analysis = case_when(
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == TRUE  ~ TRUE,
      !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE,
      TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis))
# 7. #save changes 
db <- db %>% mutate(.ok_for_analysis = case_when( !is.na(coord_qc_pass) & coord_qc_pass == FALSE ~ FALSE, TRUE ~ .ok_for_analysis), changes = append_change_tag( changes, paste0("CoordinateCleaner QA applied without sea test (validity, equal, zeros, capitals, centroids, gbif, institutions) [", Sys.Date(), "]")))

# coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
glimpse(db$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters)

# minimumElevationInMeters
range(db$minimumElevationInMeters, na.rm = TRUE)
hist(db$minimumElevationInMeters) 

# verbatimElevation
glimpse(db$verbatimElevation)

# Dates -------------

# eventDate empty 
class(db$eventDate) # as character
glimpse(db$eventDate)
unique(db$eventDate)[1:30] # some errors
db <- db %>%mutate(
    eventDate = as.character(as.Date(eventDate)),
    year = as.integer(str_sub(eventDate, 1, 4)))
# si viene de numero
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(as.numeric(db$eventDate), origin = "1899-12-30")
# si viene de otro formato
# db$eventDate <- as.Date(db$eventDate, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
glimpse(db$eventDate)

# verbatimEventDate
# empty in the raw original file

# year
glimpse(db$year)
# db$year <- as.integer(format(db$eventDate, "%Y"))
hist(db$year) # ok

# Ecology --------------

# lifeForm
unique(db$lifeForm) # can be extracted from habitat

# Specimen / collection information ----------------

# institutionCode, collectionCode, catalogNumber, recordedBy, recordNumber ok
cols <- c("institutionCode", "collectionCode", "catalogNumber", "recordedBy", "recordNumber")
# check NA
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), ~sum(is.na(.) | . == "")))
# check unique (cardinality)
db |> dplyr::summarise(across(all_of(cols), dplyr::n_distinct))
db |>
  dplyr::count(recordedBy, recordNumber) |>
  dplyr::filter(n > 1)
# has some duplicates from the same exicata but will be removed since had the same coordinates

# Record --------------------------

# basisOfRecord
unique(db$basisOfRecord) # now ok
db$basisOfRecord <- "PreservedSpecimen" # added
# db <- db %>% mutate(
#     basisOfRecord = case_when(
#       basisOfRecord == "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN" ~ "PreservedSpecimen",
#       TRUE ~ basisOfRecord))

# associatedReferences 
unique(db$associatedReferences) #empty but ok

# bibliographicCitation 
unique(db$bibliographicCitation) #non existent but ok




### CHECK LINES STEPHAN ------------------------
# check before cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))
# clean line breaks
db <- clean_linebreaks(db)
# check after cleaning
db |> dplyr::summarise(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ any(grepl("[\r\n]", as.character(.))))) |>
  dplyr::select(where(identity))





# final filter -------------------
# with only ok + epiphytes in backbone + NT
db_final <- prepare_occ_for_review(
  db,
  morrone_nt = morrone_nt,
  final_cols_order = final_cols_order
)

unique(db$datasetID)

# 1. Save processing (overwrite with enriched data)
readr::write_csv(db, file = path, na = "")

# 2. Save final processed dataset
readr::write_csv(db_final, file = "data/3_Processed_data/2_Occurrences/CUZ2025.csv", na = "")
```

```{r check_EpIG_taxo}
db %>%
  mutate(matchCodeEpig = as.character(matchCodeEpig)) %>%
  count(matchCodeEpig, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(matchCodeEpig)

db %>%
  filter(as.character(matchCodeEpig) == "0") %>%
  distinct(scientificName) %>% arrange(scientificName)
```

##### 55. Database ICESI2024

Location: \[data/2_Processing_data/2_Occurrences/ICESI2024/ICESI2024.csv\]
